answer:
Yes, there are several unofficial Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers available for Meta's Threads API, including implementations by sergekostenchuk [1], quinnjr [2], MetaThreads [3], SvetimFM [4], griffinwork40 [5], poisonstefani-dev [6], and baguskto [7].

These servers generally enable AI assistants like Claude to read posts, publish content (text, image, video, carousel), manage replies, and retrieve analytics. Setup typically requires a Meta Developer account and OAuth 2.0 credentials [2][7]. Some advanced features, such as keyword search or profile discovery, may require Meta's App Review process for production use [5].
